Disease Ontology Description: Ectopic pregnancy occurring in the fallopian tube leading to the termination of the pregnancy.
UniProt Description: Tumor necrosis factor ligand superfamily member 13
"description": Ectopic pregnancy occurring in the fallopian tube that results in the termination of pregnancy.  It typically presents with abdominal pain and vaginal bleeding.
"symptoms_description": Symptoms include abdominal pain@ vaginal bleeding@ and possible signs of shock if the fallopian tube ruptures.
"causes_description": Ectopic pregnancy is primarily caused by conditions that block or slow the movement of a fertilized egg through the fallopian tube.
"treatment_2": Laparoscopic surgery to remove the ectopic pregnancy
"prevention_1": Blood transfusion if significant blood loss has occurred
"risk_factor": Consider pre-pregnancy counseling if at risk
"family_history": History of pelvic inflammatory disease or tubal surgery
"diagnosis_methods": Presence of structural abnormalities in the fallopian tubes
"prognosis": Rupture of the fallopian tube@ hemorrhage@ infertility
column56: Relatively rare; affects approximately 1-2% of pregnancies
column57: Good with prompt diagnosis and treatment; future fertility may be affected.
